calibration.calibration_note: Recalibrated 2026-03-15 from actual V2 run times: EXQ-015 108min, EXQ-016 163min, EXQ-017 124min, EXQ-018 15min, EXQ-019 183min. Actual ms/ep-cond ~5400-6000 (8x V1 estimate). V2 substrate (HippocampalModule + real E2 + CausalGridWorld) is significantly heavier than V1.

items.title: Control Plane Precision Separation — V2 Parity
items.description: Parity check #1. Replicates V1 EXQ-013 PASS result on REE-v2 substrate (CausalGridWorld). Tests whether separating slow-timescale E1 precision updates from fast-timescale E3 policy decisions reduces hazard collision rates compared to a merged control plane. Closes SD-001 parity gap for MECH-059.
items.status_reason: Script ported from V1 2026-03-01; CausalGridWorld substitution complete

items.title: Residue Trajectory Placement — V2 Parity
items.description: Parity check #2. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-056 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether residue accumulates at trajectory positions during rollout (trajectory-wide placement) vs only the final step (endpoint-only). Validates REE invariant that harm sites are precisely localised along the planned path.
items.status_reason: Script ported from V1 2026-03-01; CausalGridWorld substitution complete

items.title: Write Locus Contamination — V2 Parity
items.description: Parity check #3. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-060/MECH-067 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether contamination is properly attributed to write loci vs residual signal bleed. Three conditions: FULL, CONTAMINATED_DURABLE, CONTAMINATED_RESIDUE. CausalGridWorld's contamination footprint makes this directly testable.
items.status_reason: Script ported from V1 2026-03-01; CausalGridWorld substitution complete

items.title: Commitment Boundary Validation — V2 Parity
items.description: Parity check #4. Replicates V1 PASS result for MECH-061 on REE-v2 substrate. Tests whether the commit boundary correctly separates pre-commit simulation errors (E2 harm predictions) from post-commit realised errors (actual env harm), and whether keeping them separate is at least as good as blending them into a single signal.
items.status_reason: Script ported from V1 2026-03-01; CausalGridWorld substitution complete

items.title: CausalGridWorld Baseline — Step 2.3 Environment Validation
items.description: Step 2.3 exit criterion validation. New V2 experiment (no V1 equivalent). Tests that CausalGridWorld produces structurally distinct agent_caused_hazard vs env_caused_hazard transitions, that the baseline REE agent creates a non-trivial contamination footprint, and that agent causal contribution exceeds the 10% threshold. PASS confirms SD-003 substrate is ready.
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-01; validates Step 2.3 prerequisites before SD-003 experiments

items.title: E1/E2 Terrain-Timescale Separation — V2 Redesign
items.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-058 on REE-v2 substrate. Exploits CausalGridWorld's two natural timescales: slow contamination terrain (E1 domain) vs fast env drift every 5 steps (policy domain). Three conditions: SEPARATED (e1_lr=5e-5, policy_lr=1e-3), COLLAPSED_FAST (both 1e-3), COLLAPSED_SLOW (both 5e-5). New metrics: contamination_avoidance_rate and post_drift_harm_rate directly test whether matched timescale separation yields functional benefit. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld gave both LRs identical convergence terrain.
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-06; redesign from EXQ-002/EXQ-006 FAIL root-cause analysis

items.title: Attribution Completion Gating — V2 Redesign
items.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-057 on REE-v2 substrate. Uses CausalGridWorld's info['transition_type'] == 'agent_caused_hazard' as a concrete self-attribution completion signal. Three conditions gate the REINFORCE return signal differently: COMPLETION_GATED (G = -agent_caused_harm only), FREE_REPLAN (G = -total_harm), ATTRIBUTION_BLIND (G = -env_caused_harm only). Tests whether gating the policy update to completed self-attribution events reduces agent-caused harm vs both mixed and attribution-absent baselines. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld had no 'action in progress' state for gating to exploit.
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-06; redesign from EXQ-004/EXQ-007 FAIL root-cause analysis

items.title: Rollout Viability Mapping
items.description: Tests ARC-018: hippocampus.rollout_viability_mapping. Asserts that E1 world-model updating from prediction-reality mismatch builds an internal viability map of trajectory sequences. VIABILITY_MAPPED condition (E1 updates every step) vs VIABILITY_FIXED (E1 frozen; policy gradient still runs). Pass requires: MAPPED slope > FIXED + 0.05 AND MAPPED last-Q harm < FIXED * 0.95.

items.title: Valence-Regime Correlation
items.description: Tests Q-007: emotion.universal_expression_channel_mapping. Asserts that internal valence (health) correlates with E3 precision modulation across resource regimes. RESOURCE_RICH (num_resources=10) vs RESOURCE_SPARSE (num_resources=1). Pass requires: RICH Pearson corr(health, precision) > 0.10 AND RICH mean_precision > SPARSE + 0.10.

items.title: Kernel Chaining Interface
items.description: Tests MECH-033: hippocampus.kernel_chaining_interface. Asserts that E2 supplies short-horizon forward-prediction kernels and hippocampal systems chain these into explicit multi-step rollouts. WITH_CHAIN (generate_trajectories + E3 select) vs NO_CHAIN (random action; E1 still learns). Pass requires: WITH_CHAIN last-Q harm < NO_CHAIN * 0.90 AND WITH_CHAIN slope > NO_CHAIN slope + 0.05.

items.title: Path Memory Ablation
items.description: Tests ARC-007: hippocampus.path_memory. Asserts that access to prior trajectory information (contamination_view + footprint_density in observation) is load-bearing for contamination avoidance. PATH_MEMORY (full obs) vs PATH_ABLATED (path-memory slice zeroed). Pass requires: MEMORY agent_caused_harm < ABLATED * 0.95 AND MEMORY total_harm <= ABLATED * 1.05.

items.title: Precision Regime Probe
items.description: Tests ARC-016: cognitive_modes.control_plane_regimes. Asserts that distinct E3 precision regimes produce distinct behavioral signatures. HIGH_REGIME (precision_max=2.0, min=1.0) vs LOW_REGIME (precision_max=0.5, min=0.1). Pass requires: |HIGH-LOW| mean precision > 0.30 AND (HIGH commit_rate < LOW - 0.05 OR HIGH last-Q harm < LOW * 0.95).

items.title: Action-Doing Mode Probe
items.description: Tests MECH-025: cognitive_modes.action_doing_mode. Asserts that the system enters a distinct action-doing mode when executing intentions with causal consequences, measurable as an E3 precision lift on agent_caused_hazard steps vs baseline. HIGH_CAUSAL (num_hazards=8) vs LOW_CAUSAL (num_hazards=1). Pass requires: HIGH precision lift > LOW lift + 0.05 AND HIGH harm_reduction_slope > LOW + 0.05.

items.title: E2 Attribution Calibration
items.description: Tests MECH-071: does trained E2.predict_harm(z_t, a_actual) calibrate higher before agent_caused_hazard transitions than env_caused_hazard transitions? Two conditions: TRAINED (200 warmup episodes) vs RANDOM (untrained E2 control). Pass: TRAINED calibration_gap > 0.05 AND |RANDOM gap| < 0.10. Prerequisite for EXQ-028 (selective residue attribution).
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-14; SD-003 substrate validated (pending EXQ-018)

items.title: Action-Loop Completion Gate (V2 Multi-Step Substrate)
items.description: Tests MECH-057a: does the action-loop completion gate reduce harm in a genuinely multi-step committed action sequence substrate? CausalGridWorld extended with subgoal_mode=True (3 waypoints, W0→W1→W2 sequence). Three conditions: FULL (gate=True, attribution=True), NO_GATE (gate=False, attribution=True), NO_ATTRIBUTION (gate=True, attribution=False). Primary metric: last-Q total harm. Pass criteria: harm[NO_GATE]/harm[FULL] >= 1.10 AND harm[NO_ATTRIBUTION]/harm[FULL] >= 1.05. Partial PASS if only criterion 1 met. Removes substrate limitation that caused EXQ-004, EXQ-007, and prior V2 re-run to fail.
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-15; subgoal_mode added to CausalGridWorld; action_loop_gate added to REEAgent. Smoke run (5 eps, 1 seed) PASS. Ready for full run.

items.title: Selective Residue Attribution
items.description: Tests MECH-072: does gating residue accumulation on E2 harm foreseeability (predict_harm > 0.3) reduce false attribution rate vs naive accumulation? Three conditions: NAIVE (all harm), FORESEEABLE (E2-gated), ORACLE (ground truth). Pass: false_attribution_rate[FORESEEABLE] < false_attribution_rate[NAIVE] AND final_harm[FORESEEABLE] <= final_harm[NAIVE] * 1.05. ORACLE provides interpretive upper bound.
items.status_reason: Script written 2026-03-14; depends on EXQ-027 calibration result
